turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <!-- Tennis ball -->
  <circle cx="335" cy="190" r="48" fill="#f6d84a" stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="4"/>
  <path d="M304 155c12 0 20 5 28 14" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M305 210c20 18 46 20 72 10" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M318 248c10-10 20-14 33-14" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M286 190c0-10 4-20 12-28" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Tennis racket (simple) -->
  <!-- Handle -->
  <rect x="168" y="300" width="22" height="140" rx="10" fill="#8a5a2b" stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="4"/>
  <rect x="160" y="312" width="38" height="22" rx="10" fill="#d3a26a" stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="3"/>

  <!-- Frame -->
  <ellipse cx="220" cy="235" rx="98" ry="70" fill="none" stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="10"/>
  <ellipse cx="220" cy="235" rx="82" ry="56" fill="none" stroke="#2b7bd6" stroke-width="4"/>

  <!-- Strings (diagonal grid) -->
  <g stroke="#1a1a1a" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.95">
    <line x1="145" y1="202" x2="296" y2="282"/>
    <line x1="155" y1="172" x2="314" y2="257"/>
    <line x1="170" y1="150" x2="330" y2="240"/>
    <line x1="182" y1="140" x2="342" y2="224"/>
    <line x1="136" y1="250" x2="283" y2="175"/>
    <line x1="148" y1="280" x2="308" y2="192"/>
    <line x1="170" y1="300" x2="330" y2="205"/>
    <line x1="198" y1="313" x2="360" y2="212"/>
    <!-- Vertical-ish strands -->
    <line x1="187" y1="175" x2="250" y2="295"/>
    <line x1="208" y1="165" x2="270" y2="288"/>
    <line x1="228" y1="160" x2="288" y2="280"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Racket head position accent -->
  <path d="M155 240c25-40 62-62 110-62" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.35"/>

  <!-- Optional shadow for depth -->
  <ellipse cx="330" cy="250" rx="90" ry="22" fill="#000" opacity="0.10"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Designing tennis concept**

I need to create a tennis concept without any letters or numbers. I think the simplest way is to draw a tennis racket and a tennis ball to represent tennis visually. I should aim for no text, just the shapes themselves. I'll use SVG shapes like circles for the ball and lines for the racket. The final design will be in a 512x512 format, and I'll make sure to return it as JSON that contains the SVG string only.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
